title: Phased-Array Ultrasonic Testing (PAUT)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical NDT/NDE literature for this principle.

E.description: Phased-Array Ultrasonic Testing (PAUT): phased array ultrasonics produces the measurement through a 5-node primitive DAG L.emit.acoustic_pulse -> S.scan.beam -> L.beamform -> L.time_of_flight -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D acoustic reflectivity. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~15; calibration-level mismatch (wedge_coupling, surface_curvature, sound_speed_uncertainty) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y(t, theta) = sum_r reflectivity(r,theta) * pulse(t - 2r/c_ref) + grain_speckle + n
E.world_state_x: Defect / strain / composition map depending on principle

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D acoustic reflectivity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 15); wedge_coupling dominates the stability cliff; surface_curvature and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [Delay-Sum, TFM] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [PAUT-Net]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |wedge_coupling| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
